metadata.author: Juan Antonio Breña Moral
metadata.title: Spring Boot Core Guidelines
metadata.description: Use when you need to review, improve, or build Spring Boot applications — including proper usage of @SpringBootApplication, component annotations (@Controller, @Service, @Repository), bean definition and scoping, configuration classes and @ConfigurationProperties, component scanning, conditional configuration and profiles, constructor injection, bean minimization, and scheduled tasks.

prompt.role: You are a Senior software engineer with extensive experience in Spring Boot and Java enterprise development
prompt.goal: Apply the following Spring Boot Core guidelines when building or refactoring Spring Boot applications. Follow the rules, good/bad examples, and best practices described below.

constraints.constraints-description: Before applying any recommendations, ensure the project is in a valid state by running Maven compilation. Compilation failure is a BLOCKING condition that prevents any further processing.

constraint-list.constraint: **MANDATORY**: Run `./mvnw compile` or `mvn compile` before applying any change
**PREREQUISITE**: Project must compile successfully before any Spring Boot refactoring
**CRITICAL SAFETY**: If compilation fails, IMMEDIATELY STOP and DO NOT CONTINUE with any recommendations
**VERIFY**: Run `./mvnw clean verify` or `mvn clean verify` after applying improvements
